title: Sunset Drive, Vol. 1
tonic_progression: Am -> C -> G -> Em -> Bm -> D -> D -> F#m -> A -> F -> Dm -> Am
narrative_arc: Twelve tracks read like a single coastal drive from late afternoon to full dark. Tracks 1-2 ease away from the pier with the sun still warm overhead; 3-5 settle into a steady coast-road cruise as the sky shifts magenta and the palms cast long shadows across the lane markers. 6-7 are the dual peak: the highway opens, the speedometer climbs, the sun bisects the horizon and the chrome on the hood goes molten orange (6 is full-throttle exhilaration; 7 is the same moment held a beat longer, vanishing-point stillness inside the speed). 8-9 step the energy down as the sun drops below the waterline and afterglow stains the underside of the clouds lavender. 10-11 are the embers — last orange sliver, taillights ahead becoming the only color left in the frame. Track 12 closes the loop: dashboard glow, ocean gone black, the opening motif played one last time, slower, an octave down. The listener should feel they have driven somewhere and arrived.

recurring_motifs.motif: Sunset arp — ascending major-7 arpeggio voiced on DX7-style FM electric piano / glassy bell pad (1-3-5-7-9, two octaves)
recurring_motifs.transformation: Track 1 plays it whole and unhurried (the album's thesis statement). Track 5 inverts it descending (the sun is now lower than the arp's starting pitch). Track 8 fragments it — only the top three notes remain, with longer rests between, as if the listener can no longer remember the full shape. Track 12 plays it whole again, one octave lower, half tempo, FM bell instead of pad: the same idea remembered at the end of the night.

recurring_motifs.motif: Highway pulse — four-note descending pad figure landing on the and-of-4 (offbeat anchor that pulls each bar forward into the next)
recurring_motifs.transformation: Track 3 introduces it under the verse as harmonic support. Track 6 brings it forward into the lead position at the peak — extended to a full 8-bar refrain, sidechained hard to the kick, the structural backbone of the apex. Track 11 returns it ghosted: same notes, same rhythm, but played on a filtered pad with the high end rolled off and the sustain bled out, as if heard through a closed car window.

tracks.title: First Light Off the Pier

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: hazy DX7 pad alone, no rhythm, slow filter sweep opening

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + filtered ascending arp (sunset motif, whole), soft sub bass on root

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: + gated reverb snare on 2 and 4, soft kick on 1, mellow analog bass walking root-fifth

sections.name: bridge
sections.instrumentation: drop to pad + arp only, breathe

sections.name: outro
sections.instrumentation: fade pad sustaining the A, drums drop out, arp once more then silence

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 84 BPM A minor, hazy DX7 pad, slow ascending major-7 arpeggio, gated reverb drums entering at 1:00, mellow analog sub bass, warm twilight nocturnal feel, late afternoon golden light off the water, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, sun still high but lowering toward the horizon, view from a wooden pier extending into a calm Pacific ocean, palm tree silhouettes on the left, magenta-orange gradient sky just beginning, chrome car parked at the pier head reflecting the sky, anticipatory mood, no people, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.composer_notes: Sets the tonic (A minor) and the album's pad palette. Sunset motif played whole, unhurried — this is the thesis. No drums until bar 32. Outro ends on Em (V of A minor) which is the relative minor of G major — but the next track moves to C major (rel maj of Am) so the listener feels the brightening lift. Don't let MusicGen sneak in a hi-hat shuffle: keep the kit minimal and gated.

sections.name: chorus2

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 88 BPM C major, warm analog pad, sidechained muted-pluck arpeggio, soft kick on every beat, gated reverb snare on 2 and 4, mellow saw lead melody, steady coastal-highway cruising feel, late afternoon golden hour, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, two-lane coast highway curving along a cliff edge, palm trees in silhouette on the right, calm ocean below, sky transitioning from gold to peach, chrome sports car mid-frame from behind driving away into the distance, neon lane markers glowing faintly, no people visible, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Relative major of A minor — the brightening lift the listener feels after track 1's Em outro chord resolves up a major third.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: +4 BPM. First step of the linear ascent.
tracks.composer_notes: First true cruiser. Sidechain on the pad must be obvious but tasteful — this is where the listener locks in. Keep the lead melody short (4-bar phrase repeated). Outro ends on G (V of C) which is also the tonic of track 3 — earned pivot.

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 92 BPM G major, four-note descending pad figure on the offbeat, sidechained warm pad, saw lead melody, gated reverb drums, kick on every beat, analog sub bass, focused coastal cruise feel, long palm tree shadows across the road, instrumental, no vocals

tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Up a perfect fifth from C — continuing the cycle-of-fifths-ascending ladder that pulls the album brighter and sharper.
tracks.composer_notes: FIRST APPEARANCE of the highway pulse motif — under the verse as harmonic support, not yet in the lead position. The breakdown at bar 41 is where the listener should first hear the motif unobstructed. Outro V chord (D) pivots cleanly to Em next (D is also bVII of Em — common rotation).

tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Relative minor of G — pivots the brightness back into a darker shade without losing the sharp-key feel. The Em is the album's first minor since the opener; the listener should feel a slight cool-edge return.
tracks.composer_notes: Energy ramps. Em is the gravity well between the bright G of track 3 and the climbing Bm of track 5. The FM bell counter-melody is a foreshadow of the sunset motif's bell-voice return in track 12.

tracks.title: Half Sun

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: pad swell + reverse cymbal up to bar 1

sections.name: verse

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 102 BPM B minor, descending major-7 arpeggio on FM bell, wide saw lead, layered atmospheric pads, gated reverb drums, hard kick, sub bass, reverse cymbal swells, sense of cresting a hill, half sun visible on the horizon, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, sun exactly half-set, bisected by a perfectly flat ocean horizon, sky a vertical gradient deep magenta at top to molten orange at the cut, palm silhouettes flanking the frame, chrome 80s sports car on a wireframe-grid road heading directly toward the sun, no people, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Up a perfect fifth from Em — completes the cycle-of-fifths-ascending ladder (Am-C-G-Em-Bm). This is the highest, sharpest minor key on the record; the next track jumps to the relative major D for the peak.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: +6 BPM (a slightly larger jump than the prior +4 step pattern — the road opens up here).
tracks.composer_notes: SUNSET MOTIF RETURNS, INVERTED. The arp now descends — the literal sun is now lower than the arp's starting pitch. This is the album's first major structural callback. 270s (45s long) — the bridge into peak deserves the extra room. Outro F# pivots into D major (F# is the major third of D) for the peak.

tracks.title: Apex

sections.name: verse

sections.name: chorus

sections.name: verse2

sections.name: chorus2

sections.name: outro

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 108 BPM D major, hard kick on every beat, sidechained layered pads, chugging arpeggio, four-note descending offbeat pulse motif in lead voice, double saw lead, gated reverb drums, white-noise risers, full-throttle coastal highway at peak speed, molten orange sun bisecting the windshield, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, view from behind a chrome sports car at full speed on a wireframe-grid highway, sun bisected on the horizon directly ahead, sky molten orange below the horizon line and deep magenta above, palm silhouettes in motion blur on either side, neon lane markers streaking past at high speed, chrome surfaces glowing, no people, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.cover_notes_internal: This is the album's hero cover — should read as the iconic image on the playlist thumbnail grid.
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Relative major of Bm. The album lifts from its sharpest minor into its brightest major — this is the structural sunrise-at-sunset moment.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: +6 BPM. The album's biggest BPM jump from track 1; the road has fully opened.
tracks.composer_notes: PEAK TRACK 1 of 2. HIGHWAY PULSE MOTIF MOVES INTO LEAD POSITION — this is its structural payoff after the foreshadow in track 3. 300s is intentional: the listener should feel they could stay here forever. The bridge at bar 65 (pulse motif solo on pad) is the album's emotional center — give it air. Don't let MusicGen pile on; restraint at the peak is what separates synthwave from EDM.

tracks.title: Vanishing Point

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: continuation feel — pad sustains D from track 6, light kick

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + shimmering arp, sub bass, brushed snare on 2 and 4

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: + ethereal lead floating above the rhythm, sidechain softer than track 6

sections.name: break
sections.instrumentation: drums drop, just pad + arp + reverb wash — vanishing point feel

sections.name: chorus2

sections.name: outro

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 112 BPM D major, shimmering arpeggio, ethereal floating saw lead, brushed gated snare, soft sidechained pads, sub bass, sense of weightless stillness at high speed, vanishing point on the horizon, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, view straight down the centerline of a wireframe-grid highway converging at a single vanishing point on the horizon, sun exactly at the vanishing point as a glowing orange disc one-third below the horizon line, sky deep magenta and violet, palm silhouettes far back on either side, chrome car nowhere visible — the listener is now driving, no people, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Same key (D major) — the only repeated tonic on the album. Tracks 6 and 7 form a dual-peak plateau; same key, different energy. Track 6 is the surge, track 7 is the same moment held a beat longer.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: +4 BPM. Highest BPM on the record.
tracks.composer_notes: PEAK TRACK 2 of 2. Stays in D major intentionally — this is the only place on the album where two consecutive tracks share a tonic. The effect is held-breath: the listener is still at altitude but the surge has settled into glide. Ending on tonic (not the dominant) is also intentional — it lets the next track (F# minor) feel like a chromatic-mediant slide downward, the first comedown step. Don't add a key change mid-track; the suspension IS the composition.

tracks.title: Disclosure Day

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: pad in F#m on a long-held note, soft kick, no melody yet — listener notices something is different before they know what

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + FRAGMENTED sunset motif (top 3 notes only, longer rests), sub bass, gated snare — the album's familiar tune cracking under the weight of the moment

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: + suspended saw lead held on long sustained notes (not sweeping), layered atmospheric pads with slow filter open

sections.name: verse2
sections.instrumentation: fragmented motif returns, lead drops out, the whole texture leans back to make room for the silence

sections.name: chorus2
sections.instrumentation: full but reverent — sidechain pulled way back, drums spacious, awe rather than impact

sections.name: outro
sections.instrumentation: fragmented motif one last time, filter close, pad sustains C# (V of F#m) — pivots to A major next as the world resumes

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 106 BPM F# minor, fragmented ascending three-note bell motif with long rests between phrases, suspended saw lead held on long sustained notes, layered atmospheric pads with slow filter sweeps, spacious gated reverb drums, lavender afterglow on the underside of clouds with something unexpected held above the horizon, awed and still and quiet, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, sun fully below horizon but the sky still glowing, undersides of stratocumulus clouds lit lavender and salmon from below, coastal highway curving to the left, palm silhouettes against a darkening sky, chrome car parked on a shoulder overlook, ocean a deep purple, no people, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.cover_notes_internal: An unidentified craft emerged from this prompt in the generated cover (track-08.png) — the SDXL run interpreted the lavender afterglow as the backdrop for a hovering object, which becomes the visual + lyrical anchor for the renamed track. Cover is the artifact; the music was retuned to match the cover, not the other way around.
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Chromatic-mediant slide down from D — F#m shares two pitches with D major (F# and A) so the descent feels related but cooler. This is the album's first comedown move.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: -6 BPM. First downward step. Symmetric mirror of the track 5 +6 ascent.
tracks.composer_notes: SUNSET MOTIF RETURNS, FRAGMENTED — recontextualized. In the original arc this was 'Afterglow Lane,' a melancholy comedown. The generated cover's emergent unidentified-object reframes the track entirely: the fragmented motif now reads as the album's familiar tune CRACKING under the weight of the moment of disclosure. Not melancholy — quieter awe. Suspended saw lead instead of wistful sweeping. Drums spacious and reverent. C# outro still pivots up a major third to A major — the world resumes in track 9, lavender-hour normalcy returning. Original 'Afterglow Lane' composition preserved as bonus track 13.

tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Chromatic-mediant up from F#m — A major shares two pitches with F#m (A and E). The lift here is subtle, almost reluctant; the album is still descending overall but the listener gets a moment of warmth before the embers.
tracks.composer_notes: Brief return to a major key on the way down. Headlights just came on — narratively this is the moment of acceptance that the day is over. Don't make it sad; make it settled. Outro E (V of A) pivots cleanly to F major next (chromatic-mediant down, a semitone-cluster move).

tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Down a major third from A — F is the chromatic-mediant of A. Cooling tones; F major's softer pitch palette suits the dimming light.
tracks.composer_notes: FM bell as lead voice is a deliberate prep for track 12's full-motif return on FM bell. Keep the mix soft — the listener should feel the album winding down without it dragging. C outro pivots to Dm (C is the bVII of Dm — natural minor relation).

sections.name: intro

sections.name: chorus

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 88 BPM D minor, four-note descending offbeat pulse motif heavily filtered with high end rolled off and short sustain, tender saw lead, layered pads with deep reverb, soft kick, sub bass, FM bell whispers, sense of last sliver of orange light vanishing below horizon, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, near-total darkness over the ocean, a single thin horizontal sliver of orange at the horizon line, sky deep violet to near-black above, palm silhouettes barely visible, chrome car taillights — two red dots — receding into the dark, no people, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Down a minor third from F major to D minor — F's relative minor. The album is now in its darkest tonal space since the opening Am.
tracks.composer_notes: HIGHWAY PULSE MOTIF RETURNS, GHOSTED. Same notes, same rhythm as track 6's peak, but heavily filtered and decayed — as if heard through a closed car window from a passing vehicle. This is the motif's farewell. Outro A (V of Dm) pivots into Am next — the album closes the ring.

tracks.title: Lights Out, Dashboard Glow

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: deep pad in Am alone, no rhythm, slow filter open over 30s

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + SUNSET MOTIF on FM bell, one octave below original, half-tempo phrasing, sub bass on root

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: + soft kick, gated snare brushed very soft, mellow lead, layered pads

sections.name: break
sections.instrumentation: drums drop, FM bell sunset motif alone with deep reverb

sections.name: chorus2
sections.instrumentation: drums return softly, lead octave-doubled, full but quiet mix

sections.name: coda
sections.instrumentation: lead drops out, FM bell sunset motif one final time whole, pad fades, drums fade, silence

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 82 BPM A minor, deep warm analog pad, FM bell ascending major-7 arpeggio played slow and full, soft kick on 1, brushed gated reverb snare, mellow saw lead, sub bass on root, sense of driving alone at night with only the dashboard glowing, nocturnal interior resolved feel, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, interior view from inside a chrome 80s sports car at night, dashboard instruments glowing soft cyan and amber, windshield showing a dark coastal highway lit only by headlights and faint stars over the ocean, palm silhouettes barely visible against a deep violet near-black sky, no people, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Down a perfect fifth from Dm to Am — closes the cycle. The album begins and ends in A minor. The full descent has been traversed.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: -6 BPM. Lowest BPM on the record, slightly below track 1's 84 BPM — the listener arrives more tired than they started, which is honest.
tracks.composer_notes: CLOSER. SUNSET MOTIF RETURNS WHOLE, ONE OCTAVE DOWN, HALF TEMPO, ON FM BELL — the same idea remembered at the end of the night. 300s long to give the coda the air it needs. The final 16-bar coda is the motif alone, then silence; do not let MusicGen add a fade-up tail or any kind of stinger. The album ends in A minor where it began — the listener should feel the ring closed.

sections.name: intro

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + FRAGMENTED sunset motif (top 3 notes only, longer rests), sub bass, gated snare

sections.name: chorus

sections.name: verse2

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 106 BPM F# minor, fragmented ascending three-note bell motif with long rests between phrases, wistful saw lead, layered atmospheric pads, gated reverb drums, sense of glowing sky after the sun has dropped, lavender afterglow on the underside of clouds, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, sun fully below horizon but the sky still glowing, undersides of stratocumulus clouds lit lavender and salmon from below, coastal highway curving to the left, palm silhouettes against a darkening sky, chrome car parked on a shoulder overlook, ocean a deep purple, no people, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Bonus track — sits outside the album's 12-track arc; not part of the cycle-of-fifths/chromatic-mediant chain.
tracks.composer_notes: BONUS TRACK. This was the originally designed track 8: a melancholy comedown with the SUNSET MOTIF returning fragmented as the listener's memory of the album's familiar tune dimming with the light. When the SDXL cover for track 8 surfaced an unidentified object in the sky, that slot was renamed 'Disclosure Day' and given a quieter, awed reading of the same music; this bonus preserves the original melancholic intent (wistful saw lead instead of suspended, normal-tight drums instead of spacious). Same musical bones, different psychic weather. Different cover seed will produce a different lavender-sky image without the UFO.

album_notes.overall_form: ABA arch over 12 tracks. A (tracks 1-5): ascent — sun lowering, road opening, tempo climbing from 84 to 102 BPM. B (tracks 6-7): dual peak in D major — track 6 is the surge (108 BPM), track 7 is the suspension (112 BPM, same key, glide instead of surge). A' (tracks 8-12): symmetric descent — chromatic-mediant slides cooling the harmony, tempo retreating 106 -> 100 -> 94 -> 88 -> 82, ending in A minor exactly where the album began. Dual peak (6+7) is intentionally off-center: the comedown gets one more track than the ascent, because the comedown is where this audience actually lives.
album_notes.key_strategy: Ascent uses cycle-of-fifths ascending through minor/major alternation: Am -> C (rel maj) -> G (P5) -> Em (rel min) -> Bm (P5). This climbs into sharper keys, brightening and tightening the harmonic palette. Peak pivots to D major (rel maj of Bm) for tracks 6 and 7 — the only repeated tonic on the album, a held-breath plateau. Descent uses chromatic-mediant slides: D -> F#m (shared F# and A) -> A major (shared A and E with F#m) -> F major (chromatic-mediant down from A) -> Dm (rel min of F) -> Am (P5 down from Dm). The descent feels related-but-cooling rather than mechanical. The ring closes: Am opens and closes the record.
album_notes.tempo_strategy: Ascent: 84 -> 88 -> 92 -> 96 -> 102 -> 108 (steps of +4 until the +6 jump at track 5 where the highway opens, then +6 again into the peak). Plateau: 108 -> 112 (+4 at the dual peak; track 7 is the album's BPM ceiling). Descent: 112 -> 106 -> 100 -> 94 -> 88 -> 82 (symmetric -6 steps mirroring the ascent's later +6 steps). Ends one tick below the opener's 84 — the listener arrives more tired than they started, which is the truth of a long drive home. Never crosses below 80 or above 118 (validation safe).
album_notes.motif_strategy: Two motifs, each with three structural appearances across the album. (1) Sunset motif — ascending major-7 arpeggio on DX7/FM bell, introduced WHOLE in track 1 (thesis), returns INVERTED in track 5 (sun lower), FRAGMENTED in track 8 (afterglow, memory dimming), and WHOLE AGAIN one octave down half-tempo on FM bell in track 12 (the same idea remembered at the end of the night). (2) Highway pulse motif — four-note descending offbeat pad figure landing on the and-of-4, introduced as harmonic support in track 3, moved into LEAD POSITION at track 6's peak as an extended 8-bar refrain, returns GHOSTED in track 11 as the listener's last view of the road. The two motifs alternate appearance points so neither overstays.
album_notes.tracklist_continuity: Each track ends on either the dominant (V) of the next track's tonic, or on a pitch that lives in the next track's tonic chord (for chromatic-mediant moves). Specifically: 1 ends on Em (the iv of A minor, but also the relative minor of G — primes track 2's C major lift). 2 ends on G (V of C, also tonic of track 3). 3 ends on D (V of G, also major-third upper-leading-tone into Em of track 4). 4 ends on B (V of Em, also tonic of track 5). 5 ends on F# (V of Bm, also major-third in D major of track 6). 6 ends on A (V of D, prepares track 7's same tonic with the held-breath plateau effect). 7 ends on D (tonic — the only tonic-ending on the album — pivoting via chromatic-mediant slide into F#m of track 8). 8 ends on C# (V of F#m, also major-third in A major of track 9). 9 ends on E (V of A, semitone-cluster pivot into F major of track 10). 10 ends on C (V of F, bVII pivot into Dm of track 11). 11 ends on A (V of Dm, also tonic of track 12 — closes the ring). The listener won't consciously hear most of these — they will only feel that each transition is inevitable rather than abrupt.
album_notes.visual_continuity_notes: Twelve covers follow the album's narrative arc as a single sunset-to-dark progression. Track 1: pier, sun still high, anticipatory. Tracks 2-3: coast road, long shadows, deepening amber. Track 4: full magenta saturation, motion blur. Track 5: half sun bisected by horizon (the album's hero structural image after track 6). Track 6: peak speed, sun bisecting windshield, molten chrome — this is the album's hero playlist-thumbnail cover. Track 7: down the centerline, vanishing point, listener now driving. Track 8: sun gone but afterglow lit clouds. Track 9: headlights just turned on. Track 10: last embers on chrome. Track 11: thin orange sliver, taillights receding. Track 12: interior view, dashboard glow, full night. The aesthetic vocabulary (palm silhouettes, magenta-to-violet skies, chrome reflections, wireframe-grid highways, neon lane markers, no people, no text) is held across all 12 — the listener scrolling the playlist thumbnails sees one cohesive record, not 12 unrelated images.
album_notes.execution_notes_for_pipeline: MusicGen is the renderer of record (license risk accepted). Every musicgen_prompt is explicit about instrumental + no vocals — do not relax this in any per-track override. Per-track durations sum to 2940s (49 minutes) for the canonical 12, +240s if track 13 is included. All BPMs are within the 80-118 validation range. Long tracks (5, 6, 7, 12) sit at 270-300s; the rest at 240s. Cover prompts are SDXL-ready, all specify 1024x1024 and 'no text, no people' to keep the SDXL pipeline from inserting either.
album_notes.bonus_track_genesis: Tracks 1-12 are the canonical album. Track 13 ('Afterglow Lane') is a bonus that preserves the originally-designed track-8 composition before the cover generation surfaced an unidentified object in the sky. That cover (track-08.png) became the visual anchor for renaming the slot 'Disclosure Day' and retuning the same musical material — fragmented motif intact, but suspended/awed instead of wistful/melancholic. Both readings of the F#m fragmented-motif idea are valid; the bonus track exists so the original creative direction isn't lost to the cover-driven retitling. Playlist convention: canonical release = tracks 1-12. Track 13 published as a standalone single on the channel, linked from the album playlist description as 'bonus / alt take of Disclosure Day.'
